definition: Ivy; an English personal name adapted to Kuku Yalanji phonology
commentary: Attested in Patz §2.3 (i) as a phonologically adapted English loan name; not in the source dictionary.
Shows loan adaptation where /b/ corresponds to English /v/ (Ivy -> aybi).

definition: Wakooka Station, a place on Princess Charlotte Bay (north of Kuku Yalanji country)
commentary: Attested in Patz Text 51 lines 2-3 and ex.(651) (Bakarrmuku-muny 'from Wakooka Station'); not in the source dictionary.
Place name; appears with ablative -muny in the texts.
Attested in Patz §3.8.2.2(i) and §3.8.3 (i); not in the source dictionary.
Trisyllabic -rri root historically based on the nominal root bijarr 'dream' but functions as an independent l-conjugation verb.
Attested in Patz §2.3 (vii) as 'biki (or bikibiki) pig'; the base form biki is not in the source dictionary (only bikibiki).
Monosyllabic English root made disyllabic by adding a final vowel.
Attested in Patz §2.4 minimal pairs (binainyji 'fig-like fruit', contrasting with binanj-ji 'with a rudder'); not in the source dictionary.
Cited as the bare noun form in the phonemic minimal-pair discussion.
Attested in Patz §2.3 (i); not in the source dictionary.
Patz notes the source is uncertain: possibly English 'benzene' or German 'Benzin' (introduced by German missionaries).
Attested in Patz ex.(356) (biringal-da 'by a scorpion', ergative); citation form biringal not in the source dictionary.
Attested in Patz §2.3 (vii); not in the source dictionary.
Monosyllabic English root 'pin' made disyllabic by inserting a semivowel sequence (pin -> biyin).
Attested in Patz ex.(392) (Yinya budmandu karrkay 'That port (suitcase) is small'); not in the source dictionary.
Attested in Patz ex.(200) (bulin-ba yijarri-ka 'put on the plate', locative); citation form bulin not in the source dictionary.
Likely an English loan ('plate') though Patz does not list it among the confirmed loans in §2.3.
Attested in Patz §3.8.5.2 (idiomatic verbal compounds) as buya-warri-y (fart(N)-run-y) 'to break wind'; not in the source dictionary.
Inseparable noun+verb compound formed with the verb root warri-y 'run'.
Attested in Patz §3.8.2.2(iv) and §3.8.3 (iv) (buyandi-l 'put in container (tr)'); not in the source dictionary.
Listed by Patz among the opaque trisyllabic l-conjugation roots.
Attested in Patz §3.8.5.2 (idiomatic verbal compounds), listed adjective+verb 'buyun-dama-l (bad-spear-l) spoil'; further attested ex.(436), and causative use 'buyun-dama-ny' ex.(72).
Idiomatic compound of buyun 'bad' + the verb root dama-l 'to spear'; inseparable, reduplicates as a whole (buyun-dama-l-dama-l, §3.8.6).
Not in the source dictionary.
Attested in Patz Text 36, line 7: 'Ngamu, dada, walnga!' 'Mother, daddy, wake up!' (mother.ABS daddy.ABS).
Familiar address term for father, distinct from the formal kin term nganjan 'father'.
Not in the source dictionary.
Attested in Patz §3.8.5.2 (idiomatic verbal compounds), body-part+verb list: 'dukul-warri-y (head-run-y) throw a tantrum'.
Idiomatic compound of dukul 'head' + the verb root warri-y 'run'; inseparable.
Not in the source dictionary.
Attested in Patz §2.3(iv),(viii) as a phonologically adapted English loanword; the variant gilaji 'glass' is also recorded in §2.3(viii)(a).
Loan from English 'glass': the consonant cluster /gl/ is broken up by an epenthetic vowel (§2.3(iv)) and the final consonant adapted by adding a vowel (§2.3(viii)).
Not in the source dictionary.

definition: to investigate, snoop on; to laugh about, ridicule someone
commentary: Attested in Patz §3.8.3.2(ii) glossed 'investigate' and §4.2.4.4 glossed 'laugh about, ridicule'; further attested in reduplicated form janjarri-l-janjarri-nya 'snoop' (ex. at §4.x).
A reinterpreted root ending in -rri (§3.8.3.2(ii)); l-conjugation transitive verb.
Not in the source dictionary.

gloss: laugh at in a friendly way
definition: to laugh at someone in a friendly way
commentary: Attested in Patz §4.2.4.4: 'Janjarri-l "laugh about, ridicule" and janjarriji-y "laugh at in a friendly way" can probably also be included in this list [of fixed intransitive stems].'
Patz explicitly regards such -ji-y forms as 'fixed' intransitive stems with meaning shifted from the transitive base, not as productive antipassives; hence dictionary-worthy as its own lexeme.
Not in the source dictionary.
Attested in Patz ex.(643) 'Waybala janyiman ... Chinese ...' and ex.(458)/(510)/(459) (also spelled janiman, e.g. ergative janiman-angka).
Borrowing referring to Chinese people, frequent in Palmer River goldrush narratives; likely from English 'Chinaman'.
Not in the source dictionary.

definition: to walk, go for a walk, go walkabout
commentary: Attested in Patz ex.(379), ex.(446), ex.(495), ex.(564), ex.(617), and Text 12 line 1; glossed 'walk-go'.

Inseparable compound of jilba 'walk' + the verb root dunga-y 'go'; citation form ends -y per the dunga-y (y-conjugation) head verb (cf. Patz §3.8.3). The candidate also surfaces uninflected as 'jilba dunga' in ex.(86).
Not in the source dictionary.
Attested in Patz §4.1.1(a), listed as a proper noun among personal names: 'Mandu, Jinabaji, Mabel (personal names)'.
Not in the source dictionary.
Attested in Patz §3.8.3.2(ii) and §3.8.3.1, glossed 'long for, crave' (cf. Djabugay jiran 'thirst, hunger').
A trisyllabic root with final -ma possibly historically the inchoative suffix, but now a fossilised, non-decomposable intransitive root (§3.8.3.2(ii)); l-conjugation.
Not in the source dictionary.
Attested in Patz §2.3 (vii) as a loanword adapted by adding a final vowel to an English root ending in a stop.
Loanword from English colloquial 'chook' (hen). Cf. reduplicated jukijuki 'chicken(s)'.

definition: An interjection used to dismiss something as nonsense or not worth heeding: 'that's nonsense, take no notice of it'.
commentary: Attested in Patz §4.10 (Interjections), glossed 'that's nonsense, take no notice of it'.
Attested in Patz §3.8.5.2 as a compound verb, glossed (burp(N)-run-y) 'to burp'.
Idiomatic compound of the noun kandarr 'burp' and the verb root warri-y 'run', a frequent compounding root.

definition: A blanket, sheet, or grass bedding spread out to lie on.
commentary: Attested in Patz ex.(153), ex.(416), ex.(563) and §2.1.1 (minimal pairs), glossed 'grass, blanket to lie on / sheet, blanket'.
Not in the source dictionary; near_headwords are unrelated kara-/Karu- forms.

definition: Place name: Roaring Meg Falls, where kija 'moon' is said to have created women.
commentary: Attested in Patz §1.2.1.8, given as kijandaku 'Roaring Meg Falls'.
Associated with the creation myth in which kija 'moon' created women at this site; contains kija 'moon'.

definition: To swim; to swim across or through water.
commentary: Attested in Patz ex.(268) 'We swam to the island' and ex.(291) 'They swam through the river', where it appears in the past form mariji-ny.
Citation form mariji-y (y-conjugation; -ny is the past suffix). A distinct 'swim' verb beside janji-l 'swim, bathe'.
Loanword from English 'mango'; English [ai] before a consonant is adapted as /ay/ in Kuku Yalanji.
Attested in Patz ex.(169) (§3.6.x causative) as the base of mumbu-bunga-l / mumbu-kanga-l 'make dirty'; not in the source dictionary.

definition: to gather, collect (e.g. nuts, plant food)
commentary: Attested in Patz ex.(363) (munja-ji-ny 'were gathered') and discussed §4.x (cf. Hershberger's muja-l); not in the source dictionary.
Verb root takes conjugation suffix -l (Patz §3.8.3).
Attested in Patz ex.(349) (jina narri-ji-ny 'stepped on (my) foot', chance passive); cf. transitive narn-ny in ex.(351). Not in the source dictionary.
Verb root takes conjugation suffix -l (Patz §3.8.3).
Attested in Patz §3.8.2.2(i), paired with the noun ngangkurr 'sound of barking'; not in the source dictionary.
Denominal -ri-y/-l verb; takes conjugation suffix per Patz §3.8.3.
Attested in Patz §3.8.5.2 compound table (also listed §3.8.x), glossed 'burst into tears' (weeping(N)-run); not in the source dictionary.
Lexicalised compound of ngirray 'weeping' + warri-y 'run'.
Attested in Patz §2.4(i) as a palatalisation example (vowel /a/ palatalised before /j/), glossed 'jealous'; not in the source dictionary.

definition: to forage; to claim or take over something that is (or is thought to be) discarded or nobody's particular property
commentary: Attested in Patz ex.(347), (372), (375); meaning glossed explicitly at §4.x ('claim/obtain something thought to be discarded'). Not in the source dictionary.
Verb root takes conjugation suffix -l (Patz §3.8.3).

definition: thoroughly, truly (doing something completely / making a thorough job of it)
commentary: Attested in Patz §3.8.6.1 Manner-particle table and ex.(192) (kuni-ny nguymal 'killed with one shot'); not in the source dictionary.
Adverbial particle; may take manner suffix -ku (nguymal-ku).
Attested in Patz ex.(477) (jarba nyanma-l-nyanma-nya 'holding on to the snake'); not in the source dictionary.
Citation form with conjugation suffix -l per Patz §3.8.3 (root nyanma + -l).
Attested in Patz §3.8.5.2 as the noun base (thought) of the compound nyunga-dama-l 'imagine something'; not in the source dictionary.
Attested in Patz §3.8.5.2 compound table, glossed 'imagine something' (thought-spear); not in the source dictionary.
Lexicalised compound of nyunga 'thought' + dama-l 'spear'.

definition: A water spirit with a female body and a fishtail, living in a spring in the Thompson Creek valley and associated with one particular Yalanji family clan; it tolerates rightful family members, who may use its spring water believed to have healing powers.
commentary: Attested in Patz §1.2.1.8 (grammar.md line 721); not in the source dictionary as this sense.
Lexicalised reduplication; distinct from the homophonous-looking near-headword 'wadal'.

definition: To cook, burn, or light (a fire); also 'boil'.
commentary: Attested in Patz at numerous examples (e.g. ex.(133), ex.(467), Text 51 line 64) and singled out in §3.8 (Table 3.15) as the sole verb root with a final root vowel /u/; not in the source dictionary.
l-conjugation, transitive.

definition: A place name in Kuku Yalanji country.
commentary: Attested in Patz §4.1.1(a) (grammar.md line 4001, listed 'Wangkabaja, Bibikarrbi (placenames)'); not in the source dictionary.
Attested in Patz §3.6.1 (grammar.md line 2790) and ex.(583); not in the source dictionary.
Based on the general interrogative root wanja but contains an opaque element -rri(y)- that Patz states cannot be analysed (§2.7, grammar.md line 2739), so treated as a distinct lexeme.

definition: To get up or rise from a sitting or lying position; to stand up.
commentary: Attested in Patz (warndiy 'get up from sitting or lying position', grammar.md line 922; §2.2.1 cites it for the rare cluster /rnd/; Text 51 lines 96-97); not in the source dictionary.
y-conjugation, intransitive.
Attested in Patz §3.8.5.2 (grammar.md line 3418, compound-verb table); not in the source dictionary.
Lexicalised compound of wawu 'breath, spirit' + yimba-l 'go straight through'; l-conjugation, transitive.
Attested in Patz §3.8.2 (grammar.md lines 2942, 2982); not in the source dictionary.
Possibly historically wubul 'many' + a verbaliser, but Patz notes the morpheme-boundary cluster /mb/ is unexplainable, so it is treated as an opaque four-syllable l-conjugation transitive root.

definition: Adverbial manner particle: (doing something) absorbed in the activity, engrossed.
commentary: Attested in Patz §3.8.6.1, manner adverbial-particle table (grammar.md line 3700); not in the source dictionary.
Attested in Patz §3.8.2.1 (grammar.md line 2943); not in the source dictionary.
Four-syllable l-conjugation transitive root.

definition: Hey! - an interjection used to attract someone's attention.
commentary: Attested in Patz §4.10 (grammar.md line 6296) and Text 51 line 8; not in the source dictionary.
A monosyllabic interjection; per §4.10 such forms may end in a glottal stop/breathy voice, lying outside normal Kuku Yalanji word phonology.

definition: this, these (demonstrative pronoun; Nyungkul absolutive form, referring to animate or inanimate, singular or plural by context)
commentary: Attested in Patz §3.7, Table 3.12 / Table 3.13 (absolutive form of the demonstrative pronoun 'this/these'); also Text 51 line 72, exx.(123),(361),(429),(431),(625),(649). Not in the source dictionary.
Nyungkul counterpart of Yalanji yanyu; suppletive inflected bases use yala- (e.g. ERG yalabu, DAT yalayngka).

definition: this, these (demonstrative pronoun; Yalanji absolutive form, referring to animate or inanimate, singular or plural by context)
commentary: Attested in Patz §3.7, Table 3.12 / Table 3.13 (absolutive form of the demonstrative pronoun 'this/these'); also §2.1.2 (initial /y/ examples), §4.1.1(d). Not in the source dictionary.
Yalanji counterpart of Nyungkul yanya; suppletive inflected bases use yalu- (e.g. ERG yalubu, DAT yaluyngku).

definition: to kill (literally 'make a corpse'); state-causative compound verb
commentary: Attested in Patz §3.8.5.1(i); not in the source dictionary.
Compound of yarkin 'corpse' (in dictionary) + state-causative verbaliser -bunga-l ('make into N'). l-conjugation. Lexicalised idiomatic meaning 'kill'.

definition: secretly; adverbial manner particle describing the manner of an action
commentary: Attested in Patz §3.8.6.1 (Manner table of adverbial particles); not in the source dictionary.
Like other adverbial particles it may take manner suffix -ku and reduplicate, but occurs in simple root form and cannot take case inflection.

definition: he/she/it (third person singular nominative); a less common free variant of nyulu
commentary: Attested in Patz §3.5.1, Table 3.6 (note): used in free variation with nyulu, which is more common. Not in the source dictionary (which has nyulu).
Closed-class personal pronoun; not a derived or inflected form.
Attested in Patz Text 51 line 4 and exx.(637),(651); not in the source dictionary.
Place name (proper noun).
Attested in Patz §3.8.5.1(i), table 'Compound + bunga-l' (jiba-wulawula 'sweet' / jiba-wulawula-bunga-l 'sweeten'); not in the source dictionary.
Lexicalised compound of jiba 'stomach/liver' + wulawula.

definition: to be sad, feel sad, grieve
commentary: Attested in Patz ex.(231) (bunjil jibabadi-y 'the widow[er] is sad') and ex.(559) (bayan-kari jibabadi-y 'the homeless one is sad'); not in the source dictionary.
Y-conjugation intransitive verb (citation = non-past per §3.8.3); lexicalised compound jiba 'stomach/liver' + badi 'cry'.

definition: to look back, turn and look behind oneself
commentary: Attested in Patz Appendix Text 3, line 19 (kurrka-nyaji-ji-ny 'looked back'); not in the source dictionary.
Lexicalised reflexive compound kurrka 'back' + nyaji 'see' + reflexive/general-intransitive -ji.
Attested in Patz ex.(67a): luku-bulal '(a) pair of trees' (tree + dual/pair), glossed 'A pair of trees is standing there'; not in the source dictionary.
Attested in Patz §3.4 / §6.4 discussion as muduwajuny 'at daybreak' (a time word, alongside wunkuny 'next day') and in Appendix Text 36 lines 24, 33 (muduwaju-ny); not in the source dictionary.
Entered as a temporal noun: the harvested 'become dawn' verb reading is doubtful because u-final intransitive (y-conjugation) verb roots are unattested (Patz §3.8.3.1, Table 3.15).

definition: to fool around, play about, dance in a funny/playful way
commentary: Attested in Patz ex.(433): nguju-wuri-y 'funny-dance-IMP' ('Don't play/fool around with the spear...'); not in the source dictionary.
Lexicalised compound nguju 'fun, funny' + wuri-y 'dance' (a y-conjugation intransitive verb per §3.8.3), so cited as nguju-wuri-y.
Attested in Patz ex.(69): ubirri-warra '(Mossman)-inhabitant.ABS' ('The Mossman people went to the Bloomfield people'); not in the source dictionary.
Place name; the inhabitant suffix -warra marks 'people of Mossman'.
